steps.instruction: Add an SR latch, two InputSwitches, and two LEDs to the canvas
steps.hint: Use the Memory tab to add an SR latch. Then use the I/O tab to add two InputSwitches and two LEDs.

steps.instruction: Connect the InputSwitches to the S and R inputs of the SR latch. Then connect the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs
steps.hint: Connect one InputSwitch to the Set (S) input and the other to the Reset (R) input. Connect each output of the latch to a different LED.

steps.instruction: Toggle the InputSwitches and observe how the SR latch stores its state. Setting the S input to 1 causes the Q output to become 1, while setting the R input to 1 resets Q to 0. When both inputs return to 0, the latch remembers its last state until it is set or reset again
Remove the SR latch while keeping the InputSwitches and LEDs. Then add a D latch
steps.hint: The D latch stores a single input value and avoids the invalid input combination of an SR latch.

steps.instruction: Connect one InputSwitch to the D input and the other to the Enable (E) input. Connect the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs
steps.hint: The D input is the value to be stored. The Enable input determines when the latch updates its stored value.

steps.instruction: Toggle the InputSwitches and observe the D latch. When Enable is 1, the output follows the D input. When Enable is 0, the latch remembers its previous value
